rationale: The citing paper focuses on semantic/emotional attention capture in cluttered advertising (headline emotionality, orienting eye shifts, brand awareness), whereas the cited paper is about conjoint-based preference measurement for really new products using mental simulation/analogy. There is no evident overlap in focal constructs or methods in the notes, and the matched evidence is only a bare DOI reference (no contextual citation text), so this looks like an incidental/background mention rather than substantive use.
